failure_modes.text: Cheap symbolic signals can substitute for real capability, accountability, or truth-aligned behavior.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when cheap symbolic signals are allowed to substitute for real capability, accountability, or truth-aligned behavior.
failure_modes.mitigation: Audit signals for substance: require every symbolic claim to be backed by a verifiable capability, commitment, or track record.

failure_modes.text: Message optimization can hide a weak underlying offer when impression management outruns substance.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when the message is optimized for impression management while the underlying offer, reasoning, or execution standard remains weak.
failure_modes.mitigation: Test whether the message would survive due diligence: before sending a signal, ask what a skeptical recipient would check and whether the underlying substance would hold up.

failure_modes.text: Signaling fails when a message is accurate in the abstract but too useless to guide concrete action.
failure_modes.mitigation: Make signals actionable: translate abstract positioning into concrete guidance that a recipient can use to make a decision or take a next step.

premortem_questions.text: What costly behavior would prove this signal is real rather than symbolic?
If the underlying offer stayed the same, would this message still persuade mainly because it looks good?
What concrete decision or action should this signal change for the receiver?
premortem_questions.source_quote: it fails to help the recipient make decisions

heuristics.text: Use signals that are costly enough or behaviorally concrete enough to separate appearance from substance.
Translate strategy into action-legible communication so the receiver knows what to do, not just what to admire.
Treat impression quality as a diagnostic clue, not as proof that the underlying capability is real.
heuristics.source_quote: appearance-trustworthy behavior rather than be-trustworthy behavior

curation_notes.summary: The source is naturally chunk-ready on costly credibility, anti-impression-management discipline, and action-legible messaging.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor persuasion sprawl because the raw source is stronger on credibility diagnostics and truth-aligned signaling.
open_questions: The first premortem item compresses several source passages on costly signaling so the chunk stays operational.

allies: The raw ally surface is strongest on incentives, principal-agent-problem, abstraction, and optionality-like commitment pressure as later relation material.
antagonists: The raw antagonist pressure is strongest on confirmation-bias and low-substance impression games, but only clean current-vocabulary ids should survive Wave 3.
structured_tensions: The source has explicit tension blocks with incentives and confirmation-bias that cleanly map into the current target-id surface.
